description: Two-state pressed button (on / off). Radix renders a <button> with aria-pressed reflecting the current state. Used for toolbar-style toggles like Bold, Italic, or show / hide controls. Unlike Switch, it's not meant for settings; it's for a visual "pressed" affordance on a control that immediately changes something.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to / from the toggle.

focus.notes: Uses focus-visible:ring-1. When pressed, data-[state=on]:bg-accent provides the pressed visual.

announcements: Announces as a button with "pressed" / "not pressed" state.
Accessible name comes from the button's text or aria-label (icon-only toggles must add aria-label).
screenReader.notes: Because Toggle renders a button with aria-pressed, screen readers describe state in terms of pressed, not checked; that's correct for toolbar toggles but may confuse users expecting a checkbox pattern. Use Switch instead for settings that should read as on / off.

contrast.text: Default variant uses foreground; outline variant adds a border. Pressed state uses text-accent-foreground on bg-accent. All meet WCAG AA
contrast.ui: Focus ring uses ring-ring; meets AA non-text contrast

motion.reducedMotion: Only a color transition on hover / state change; no motion-based animations

consumerResponsibilities: Provide an accessible name for icon-only toggles via aria-label.
Use Toggle for visual / inline controls (toolbar buttons); use Switch for persistent settings.
For mutually-exclusive sets of toggles, use ToggleGroup instead of independent Toggles; it handles the radio-like semantics.
